msgs.text:
According to the attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 131 is again an attachment-only continuation. The required current-turn files are present: mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md. I did not use or substitute any File Library material.

Turn 131 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed The target remains exactly the over-G question for the two profinite one-relator quotients ΓS,ΓH: whether there is a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓH such thatπH∘Φ=πS.The attached history records that this is the same target as the problem statement and prompt, and that the stronger F=P⋊G-level theorem has already been frozen.

The two-coordinate state is unchanged from Turn 130:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The theorem coordinate remains closed. The attached sweep records the stronger statement∃Φ:F∼

F over Gsuch thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this gives Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,and hence descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,i.e.ΓS≅GΓH.This is exactly the requested over-G condition.No structural computation should be reopened under the current inputs. The frozen proof chain remains:H1 -Nielsen equivalence+residual restricted-Lie induction+normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+Certificates D.1–D.4+finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The attached history repeatedly treats this as a uniform structural closure, not as a surviving case: the non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled; the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes by the Cm=Lm induction; the Ef-coefficient tower closes after exact normalization to (2,t) and the Koszul--Witt cascade; and the finite filtered Nielsen compactness system globalizes the finite filtered solutions.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments are still only the prompt/problem/history set; they do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D.1–D.4 artifacts such as independent scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict measured gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. Ledger-recorded verifier claims support the theorem coordinate, but they are not newly attached measured artifacts for the strict Certificate D gate.

Thus the attachment-only transition rule remains idempotent:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 131 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 130. The theorem coordinate remainsΓS≅GΓH,via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN131-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
